_comment: MOCKUP/DESIGN data. Quotes taken from the public RBI documents (see each source link), re-verified 2026-06-12. RBI is the HONEST TWO-LAYER case: a binding generic crypto rule today + a forward-looking study committee. There is NO binding RBI post-quantum mandate yet — this page must not imply one.
title: RBI — Cryptographic Controls (binding today) & Quantum Readiness (Q-SAFE, forward-looking)
summary: RBI has NO post-quantum mandate today. What is binding now is a generic 'use strong cryptography' rule, which CryptaMap evidences. RBI's quantum work is a study committee (Q-SAFE) whose terms of reference describe a CBOM — promising, but it is a study body with no obligation yet. This page separates the two layers so neither is overstated.

documentsHeld.title: Master Direction on IT Governance, Risk, Controls and Assurance Practices
RBI sets up Committee on Quantum-Safe Cryptography (Q-SAFE) — Press Release

claims.headline: BINDING TODAY — cryptographic controls must be strong (generic, not PQC-specific)
claims.quote: The key length, algorithms, cipher suites and applicable protocols used in transmission channels, processing of data and authentication purpose shall be strong …
claims.where: Master Direction RBI/2023-24/107, §16 Cryptographic controls (effective 1 Apr 2024)
claims.cryptaMapNote: This is binding on RBI-regulated entities today. CryptaMap directly evidences it: it inventories key lengths, algorithms, cipher suites and TLS versions across the AWS org and flags deprecated/weak ones. NOTE: this clause says nothing about quantum/PQC — it is general crypto hygiene, and that is exactly how this page presents it.

claims.headline: FORWARD-LOOKING — Q-SAFE committee will study a CBOM (study body, NO obligation yet)
claims.quote: Evaluate the financial sector's cryptographic inventory through a Cryptography Bill of Materials (CBOM), assess crypto agility and identify the critical systems and processes most vulnerable to such threats.
claims.where: Q-SAFE Committee Terms of Reference, RBI Press Release PR 2026-2027/325 (25 May 2026)
claims.cryptaMapNote: This describes CryptaMap's exact output (a CBOM + crypto-agility assessment) — but it is the mandate of a STUDY COMMITTEE whose report is pending. It creates NO obligation on regulated entities today. Treat as a strong signal of direction, not a current requirement.

claims.headline: OTHER (non-PQC) — .bank.in domain migration is an anti-phishing measure
claims.quote: … banks shall migrate to the '.bank.in' domain … not later than October 31, 2025.
claims.cryptaMapNote: Listed only to prevent confusion: this is a domain-trust / anti-phishing deadline with ZERO cryptographic or post-quantum content. CryptaMap does not address it and it is not part of any PQC narrative.

honestCaveats: RBI has NOT issued a post-quantum mandate. Do not read this page as 'RBI requires PQC' — it does not (as of the verification date).
The only binding crypto rule here is generic ('use strong cryptography'); the quantum/CBOM language is a study committee's terms of reference, not a regulation.
CryptaMap is evidence, not a compliance certification. Verify against the latest RBI circular before audit use.
